title: Captured, imprisoned and tortured: Russia 'ignores all laws and does whatever it wants' to Ukrainian civilians
subhead: Since the 2022 invasion, Moscow has been arresting citizens from the occupied territories en masse in an effort to crush all resistance. More than 16,000 people are currently imprisoned, according to the Ukrainian Defender of Rights.

title: Israel PM says close to killing all architects of October 7 attack

New Hungarian PM discloses Viktor Orban's luxurious offices
subhead: Péter Magyar, the recently appointed prime minister of Hungary, launched a media campaign on Friday, signaling a break with his far-right predecessor, who was notorious for allowing corruption to flourish among his inner circle.

title: Cannes: Exiled Russian filmmaker Andrey Zvyagintsev's 'Minotaur' delivers anti-war message through intimate lens
subhead: The 62-year-old filmmaker, now living in France, chose the conflict between his country and Ukraine as the backdrop for his latest film. He depicts the consequences of the war, which he openly condemns, within the realm of married life. Despite his open hatred of Putin's regime, his mere presence at Cannes could draw condemnation from Ukrainian advocacy groups.

title: Ligue 1: PSG defeated by Paris FC ahead of Champions League final
subhead: Paris Saint-Germain lost 2-1 on Sunday ahead of the Champions League final on May 30 against Arsenal. Ousmane Dembélé came off before the half-hour in an apparent injury scare. Coach Luis Enrique said 'it has been a tricky end to the season' for PSG as they prep for 'the biggest match in our history.'

title: Alya Aglan, historian: 'De Gaulle is difficult to interpret; he is himself an actor'
subhead: More than half a century after his death, General de Gaulle continues to inspire filmmakers, cartoonists and comic book authors. In an interview with Le Monde, historian Alya Aglan unpacks the driving forces behind these diverse portrayals.

subhead: In Corsica, chess players began learning the game since they were children, with the island counting 16 times more registered players per capita than the rest of France.

title: A conversation with Peter Jackson, the genius tinkerer turned blockbuster master
subhead: The director of 'The Lord of the Rings,' who was honored with an honorary Palme d'Or, reflects on his beginnings as a self-taught filmmaker in New Zealand.

title: From Paris to China, Bistrot Ordinaire takes diners on a surprising culinary journey
subhead: In the 11th arrondissement of Paris, self-taught chef Liang Shan has opened a bistro that is 'ordinaire' in name only. His menu boldly bridges Chinese and French cuisines, far from your typical Parisian fare.
